violations.description: Timestamp-based signatures use Date.now() across suites, which can create clock-sensitive behavior in edge CI conditions.
violations.suggestion: Prefer deterministic time fixtures where feasible, or centralize timestamp creation behind a helper with test-time injection.

violations.description: Random token generation via randomUUID() reduces strict reproducibility for failure replay.
violations.suggestion: Use deterministic test id helpers similar to deterministicToken(testInfo, ...) for all generated IDs.

violations.description: ATDD E2E signature headers depend on runtime timestamp and pseudo-signature values.
violations.suggestion: Switch to deterministic signed-header helper shared with automate suites when unskipping scenarios.

recommendations: Standardize on deterministic ID/time helper utilities in all E1 specs
Consolidate signature timestamp generation behind a reusable helper with injectable time source
Align ATDD E2E signing helpers with automate suites before enabling skipped tests
summary: E1 tests are mostly deterministic but still include clock/random inputs in helper setup paths.
